meaning: n.预感,先兆,预兆adj.预感的,预兆的,不祥之兆的
examples: She had a sense of foreboding that the news would be bad.
His triumph was overshadowed by an uneasy sense of foreboding.
The sailor's wife had a foreboding that he would not return.
In minutes, the sky would turn from a perfect blue to a foreboding gray.
We pushed the start button and felt the foreboding rumble of the W-12 engine beneath us.
